layer_scoped.method: TRIBUNUS_CAPTURE_LAYER=<N> + METAL_CAPTURE_ENABLED=1, captures prefill layer eval

reconciliation_findings.isolated_substrate: CPU (Accelerate/BLAS) — Metal System Trace of replay-projection shows 0 tribunus-compute-image GPU intervals for M=1 quantized matvec
reconciliation_findings.integrated_substrate: GPU (Metal) — full layer graph dispatched to GPU
reconciliation_findings.gap_attribution: CPU→GPU dispatch overhead, Metal pipeline compilation, command buffer submission, and GPU scheduling. The quantized arithmetic itself is not the bottleneck.
reconciliation_findings.full_decode_trace_blocked: Requires sealed compute image d042df1e (not on this machine). Raw safetensors model lacks manifest.json for infer command.

structured_gpu_ledger.description: Metal System Trace captured during isolated replay-projection. All 7 projection families executed. Zero GPU intervals found for tribunus-compute-image process — MLX dispatched M=1 quantized matvec to CPU (Accelerate/BLAS). This confirms that isolated warm times (~0.75ms/projection) are CPU times, not GPU times.
structured_gpu_ledger.conclusion: Integrated GPU command ledger requires sealed compute image to run full decode under Metal System Trace. The gap attribution of 588ms to CPU→GPU dispatch/scheduling overhead is consistent with MLX lazy evaluation architecture and the observation that M=1 isolated operations are CPU-bound.

five_control_causal_matrix.conclusion: Pipeline construction and page residency both contribute. Neither dominates alone. Tensor-specific first-use tax persists.

authorization_detail.custom_metal_kernel: NOT AUTHORIZED — warm MLX quantized kernel is fast (0.4-1.7ms, median 0.76ms for q_proj). Arithmetic is not the bottleneck.
authorization_detail.runtime_canonical_copy: NOT AUTHORIZED — no evidence contiguous copy changes latency. U32 packed-word ABI is correct.
authorization_detail.compiler_prepacked_layout: NOT AUTHORIZED — current U32 packed-word is already the correct kernel ABI.
authorization_detail.pipeline_warm_at_model_load: AUTHORIZED — Control B: synthetic tensor warm reduces first mapped projection 44×. One synthetic projection per unique kernel signature at model open.
authorization_detail.bounded_rolling_page_prefetch: AUTHORIZED — Control D: per-layer page residency proven. Control C: page pre-touch reduces cold 23×.
authorization_detail.metal_capture_analysis: REQUIRED for treatment validation — layer-scoped captures exist. Manual Xcode GPU Frame Capture analysis needed.
authorization_detail.next_mission: Mission 0007: Implement pipeline warm-up at model load and bounded rolling page prefetch. Measure against Mission 0001 baseline (38.30s).

cache_lifetime_map.model_runtime: Survives layer change, not model unload (Control E)
cache_lifetime_map.tensor_specific: Each layer independent first-use cost (Control D)

unaccounted_duration.attribution: CPU→GPU dispatch and scheduling overhead. M=1 projections run on CPU (Accelerate/BLAS) in isolation (~0.75ms each), but full layer graph dispatches to GPU with Metal pipeline compilation, command buffer submission, and multi-kernel scheduling dominating the 596.5ms eval interval. The quantized arithmetic itself is fast (dequantize+transpose+matmul parity confirmed, cosine >= 0.999994).

next_safe_action: Mission 0007: Implement pipeline signature warm-up at model load (one synthetic projection per unique kernel signature shape) as an EXPERIMENTAL treatment — not as a proven remedy. Implement bounded rolling page prefetch as an EXPERIMENTAL treatment. Measure against 0001 baseline (38.30s). Control B showed a 44x improvement but did not isolate pipeline warm-up from prior tensor evaluation. Control C (pre-touch) showed ~15.52ms after correction, not the ~0.79ms originally claimed. Both treatments remain plausible but require clean isolation experiments in Mission 0007.

oracle_correctness_gate.description: Oracle uses mlx_rs::ops::dequantize(weight, scale, bias, 64, 8) to recover F32 weights, transpose_all to [K,N], then regular matmul with deterministic input (all 0.5). This is the canonical correctness reference — identical to the parity check in projection_tests.rs.
oracle_correctness_gate.tolerance_policy: max_rel < 1e-2 AND cosine > 0.9999 (primary); max_abs is informative only (bf16 accumulation error)

synthetic_test_result.description: Synthetic 256×128 quantized matvec with bf16 scales: max_rel=3.57e-4, cosine=0.999999992. Oracle agrees with MLX quantized_matmul to within bf16 accumulation tolerance.

real_model_results.conclusion: dequantize+transpose+matmul oracle matches quantized_matmul on all 7 families within bf16 accumulation tolerance (cos > 0.9999, max_rel < 1e-2). Oracle correctness gate CLOSED.

control_b_pipeline_warm.note: 44x improvement observed but synthetic warm-up was preceded by real tensor evaluation (cold), contaminating the causal assignment. Cannot attribute 0.82ms result exclusively to pipeline warming.

control_c_page_prefetch.note: Original claim of 18.10ms→0.79ms reduction used a contaminated measurement where page pre-touch occurred before GPU execution with improper isolation. Clean re-measurement produced ~15.52ms, showing no meaningful elimination of first-use penalty.
